title: Soul Train

jokes.text: Twenty-one-eighteen, I win. Hey, you wanna play again? Same rules as last game? Yeah, sure. Zero-eighteen.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks to play again with same rules after losing
jokes.punchline: Will immediately starts the score at 0-18, giving Carlton the same deficit
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and economy. The punchline is perfectly timed and reveals Will's cleverness.

jokes.text: I have a date. With whom? Same lady I see every Saturday night. Dr. Quinn, Medicine Woman.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims he has a date and builds anticipation about who it's with
jokes.punchline: It's a TV character - he's watching a show, not dating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - earnest delivery of something pathetic. The specific show reference adds character depth.

jokes.text: I mean, come on. You're pretty, you're fun, you're intelli... Vision.
jokes.setup: Will starts to compliment Hilary's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Catches himself and switches to 'vision' - implying she's not intelligent but has good eyesight
jokes.explanation: Quick save that's both diplomatic and insulting. The hesitation and recovery is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I'm a beautiful woman trapped in an even more beautiful woman's body.
jokes.setup: Hilary complains about being a beautiful woman trapped
jokes.punchline: She's trapped in an even MORE beautiful woman's body - escalating her own vanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the 'trapped in the wrong body' trope. Pure Hilary narcissism delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Didn't none of them kids look like me, did they, Uncle Phil? I was just checking, man, just checking.
jokes.setup: Will asks if any kids at the sleepover looked like him
jokes.punchline: His immediate 'just checking' reveals he's paranoid about Uncle Phil's past
jokes.explanation: Plays on Will's street-smart awareness and Uncle Phil's past. The defensive 'just checking' sells it.

jokes.text: This isn't gonna take long, is it? I have a date. We know.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions his date again
jokes.punchline: Will's flat 'we know' shows everyone's tired of hearing about Dr. Quinn
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that works because of the deadpan delivery and family dynamic.

jokes.text: Where you all get married at, The Flip Wilson Show?
jokes.setup: Learning Uncle Phil proposed on Soul Train
jokes.punchline: Will escalates by asking if they got married on another vintage variety show
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke with specific cultural reference that fits Will's character and the generational gap.

jokes.text: [IN DEEP VOICE] You can bet your last money it's gonna be a stone gas, honey. And until next time, love, peace and... Soul!
jokes.setup: Will pulls out a Don Cornelius action figure
jokes.punchline: The toy does a perfect impression of the real Don Cornelius catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy combined with impersonation. Works because it shows Will's genuine love for Soul Train.

jokes.text: You played with a doll? Ain't... Ain't no doll, man, it's an action figure.
jokes.setup: Carlton calls Will's Don Cornelius toy a doll
jokes.punchline: Will defensively insists it's an action figure, like a kid defending G.I. Joe
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag while showing Will's embarrassment about his childhood attachment.

jokes.text: Afro pick sold separately.
jokes.setup: Continuing the action figure bit
jokes.punchline: References the classic toy marketing disclaimer with period-appropriate accessory
jokes.explanation: Clever detail that extends the toy parody with era-specific humor. Economical and well-observed.

jokes.text: You was like a real cool, hip, handsome dude back then, man. What happened?
jokes.setup: Will compliments Uncle Phil's past coolness
jokes.punchline: Asks what happened - implying Phil is no longer cool, hip, or handsome
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline with perfect timing. The compliment makes the insult hit harder.

jokes.text: No slap in the head or nothing. I'm scared.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil doesn't react to Will's insult with his usual physical punishment
jokes.punchline: Will is disturbed by the lack of normal response - it's scarier than being hit
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectations by showing Will prefers Uncle Phil's normal angry reactions. Great character insight.

jokes.text: Carlton, what's wrong, man? Are you having a Maalox moment?
jokes.setup: Carlton is acting strangely
jokes.punchline: Will asks if he's having a 'Maalox moment' - treating stress like a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific observation about Carlton's uptight nature and tendency toward stomach problems.

jokes.text: Two things, Carlton. One, that's not dancing. Two, I would appreciate it if you stayed away from the show.
jokes.setup: Will announces he has two criticisms of Carlton
jokes.punchline: First insults Carlton's dancing, then reveals his selfish motive for keeping Carlton away
jokes.explanation: Good structure with the numbered format leading to Will's true motivations.

jokes.text: Again, two things, Carlton. One, because you say stuff like 'pray tell.'
jokes.setup: Carlton asks why he should stay away using fancy language
jokes.punchline: Will uses the same 'two things' format to mock Carlton's pretentious vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the format while highlighting the cultural/class divide between the characters.

jokes.text: Well, let me break it down for you, Teeny Petito.
jokes.setup: Will is going to explain something to Carlton
jokes.punchline: Calls him 'Teeny Petito' - a play on 'petit' meaning small, emphasizing Carlton's height
jokes.explanation: Creative nickname that plays on Carlton's size while sounding like a real name. Good wordplay.

jokes.text: It's not a doll, man, it's an action figure.
jokes.setup: Carlton again calls it a doll
jokes.punchline: Will repeats his defensive correction, showing his embarrassment continues
jokes.explanation: Repetition of the earlier gag. Works as a running joke but lacks novelty.

jokes.text: And you got no business even being on the Soul Hyundai let alone the Soul Train.
jokes.setup: Will is insulting Carlton's worthiness for Soul Train
jokes.punchline: Says he's not even good enough for 'Soul Hyundai' - a cheaper, less prestigious version
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that creates a hierarchy of soul credibility. Original and perfectly delivered insult.

jokes.text: Me? You're the embarrassment, you urban urchin.
jokes.setup: Will calls Carlton an embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Carlton fires back with 'urban urchin' - overly formal insult that shows he's trying too hard
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - tries to be street but uses SAT vocabulary. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Carlton Banks, soul brother number one.
jokes.setup: Carlton is defending his soul credentials
jokes.punchline: Declares himself 'soul brother number one' - completely delusional given his obvious lack of soul
jokes.explanation: Absurd self-declaration that perfectly captures Carlton's delusion about his coolness.

jokes.text: Sweetheart, I think the suit's the same size. I said it must have shrunk a little.
jokes.setup: Phil suggests his suit is the same size (implying he's gained weight)
jokes.punchline: Vivian firmly insists the suit shrank - making Phil backtrack immediately
jokes.explanation: Classic married couple dynamic - husband learns to agree rather than argue about weight.

jokes.text: A chili cook-off.
jokes.setup: Will asks what could be more important than Soul Train
jokes.punchline: Phil's answer of 'chili cook-off' is so mundane it's absurd compared to Soul Train
jokes.explanation: The mundane nature of the conflict makes Phil's priorities seem absurd. Good contrast.

jokes.text: Uh... Besides, my feet hurt... and, well, I'm afraid my head will look shiny on camera.
jokes.setup: Phil tries to justify avoiding Soul Train
jokes.punchline: Increasingly petty and vain excuses
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's vanity and insecurity about aging. Character-appropriate escalating excuses.

jokes.text: If that's a groove, the Grand Canyon's a ditch.
jokes.setup: Dance instructor calls Phil's dancing a 'groove'
jokes.punchline: Carlton compares it to calling the Grand Canyon a ditch - massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that perfectly captures how bad Phil's dancing must be. Strong structure and memorable.

jokes.text: Look, Will, I'm not gonna have some platformed freak make a fool out of me.
jokes.setup: Phil is angry at the dance instructor
jokes.punchline: 'Platformed freak' - specific insult about 70s fashion that shows Phil's prejudices
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's stuck-up attitude and discomfort with 70s culture. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: I danced at Sam Cooke's funeral.
jokes.setup: Lamont is defending his credentials as a dancer
jokes.punchline: Claims he danced at a funeral - completely inappropriate but delivered as a credential
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and absurd. The idea of dancing at a funeral as a resume item is brilliant.

jokes.text: Overgrown Isaac Hayes-looking fool.
jokes.setup: Lamont is insulting Phil as he leaves
jokes.punchline: Compares Phil to Isaac Hayes - another large, bald Black man with a distinctive look
jokes.explanation: Physical comparison insult that's specific to the era. Decent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'd build a fence to keep you away. One with barbed wire and maybe a sniper up on the roof to pick you off just in case you got over the fence. And given the slightest chance that he'd miss, there'd be two rottweilers guarding my problem.
jokes.setup: Will asks what Phil would do if Will got near his problem
jokes.punchline: Phil describes increasingly elaborate security measures to keep Will away
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that builds absurdity while showing how much Will annoys Phil. Well-structured buildup.

jokes.text: You don't want my help, do you? I'll go.
jokes.setup: Phil just described elaborate measures to keep Will away from his problems
jokes.punchline: Will understates the obvious - that Phil doesn't want his help - and leaves
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement after Phil's elaborate rant. The economy and timing make it work.

jokes.text: Get that damn flashlight out of my face!
jokes.setup: Will is doing his Don Cornelius impression
jokes.punchline: Breaks character to complain about Carlton's flashlight - ruins the impression
jokes.explanation: Good breaking of the fourth wall moment that shows the reality behind Will's performance.

jokes.text: Action figure, action figure. Is that so hard to understand?
jokes.setup: Carlton calls it a doll again
jokes.punchline: Will's exasperated repetition shows this argument has been going on
jokes.explanation: Third repetition of the gag. Shows Will's frustration but is getting stale.

jokes.text: Carlton, he's not hurting her.
jokes.setup: Carlton says he's been listening outside his parents' bedroom
jokes.punchline: Will assumes Carlton is worried about domestic violence, not arguments
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Will's street background making him assume the worst.

jokes.text: Sorry, I got caught up in the fantasy. About me? No, about that decent job.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks if Geoffrey would ask her out if he had a decent job, Geoffrey pauses
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey clarifies he was fantasizing about having a decent job, not dating Hilary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey response - dry, insulting, and completely unexpected. Great misdirection and character work.

jokes.text: I don't have a date. Must you throw that in my face?
jokes.setup: Ashley asks what Hilary would do if a guy tried to kiss her on a date
jokes.punchline: Hilary breaks down because the question reminds her she doesn't have dates
jokes.explanation: Shows Hilary's vulnerability beneath her vanity. Character-driven but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, no, he was clipping his toenails, I took one in the eye.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv asks what's wrong with Will
jokes.punchline: She assumes it's about the parents fighting, but it's actually about getting hit by a toenail clipping
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with disgusting but funny physical comedy. Well-timed and unexpected.

jokes.text: Which is kind of difficult to do when you go there with somebody old. Oh, I mean... I mean that's kid talk, Aunt Viv. Bad means good. And stupid means great. And old means beautiful.
jokes.setup: Will calls Aunt Viv old and immediately realizes his mistake
jokes.punchline: Tries to cover by claiming 'old means beautiful' in kid slang, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Will digs himself deeper trying to fix his insult.

jokes.text: You are so old. You are the oldest person I ever seen.
jokes.setup: Will is trying to use 'old' as a compliment
jokes.punchline: Doubles down on calling her old, making the 'compliment' even more obviously insulting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the situation more absurd. Will's logic is hilariously flawed.

jokes.text: Is that your aunt? Oh, yeah, but we see other people. What a freak.
jokes.setup: Woman asks if that's Will's aunt
jokes.punchline: Will pretends they're dating and claims they 'see other people' - woman calls him a freak
jokes.explanation: Clever way for Will to hit on someone while making a joke about his situation. Backfires perfectly.

jokes.text: Please, Miss Ashley, don't give me away.
jokes.setup: Ashley discovers Geoffrey at Soul Train
jokes.punchline: The stuffy British butler is secretly attending the hippest show on TV and begging not to be exposed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character subversion. Geoffrey having a secret soul side is both unexpected and perfect.

jokes.text: Don't touch me.
jokes.setup: Carlton is presumably doing his awkward dancing on Soul Train
jokes.punchline: Someone tries to touch him and he recoils - his dancing is so bad it's repulsive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his social awkwardness on full display. Economy makes it work.

jokes.text: [SCREAMS]
jokes.setup: Carlton is dancing on the Soul Train line
jokes.punchline: He screams - presumably falling or failing spectacularly at dancing
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to Carlton's dancing attempts. Predictable but fits the character.

tmdb_overview: At a Soul Train reunion, middle-aged Philip feels like his train has left the station.
